Create .gitignore with this exact content:

Create rust-toolchain.toml:

    [toolchain]

    components = ["rustfmt", "clippy"]

Create .editorconfig:

    root = true

    [*]
    charset = utf-8
    end_of_line = lf
    insert_final_newline = true
    indent_style = space
    indent_size = 4
    trim_trailing_whitespace = true

    [*.md]
    trim_trailing_whitespace = false

Create LICENSE: paste the standard Apache License 2.0 text verbatim. Use "2026 Radoslav Tsvetkov <author>" as the copyright line.

Create README.md:

    # Memora

    Verifiable cognitive memory for personal Obsidian-compatible vaults.
    Rust + SQLite + MCP-native. Single binary. Local-first.

    **Status: pre-v0.1, in development.**

    ## Pitch

    Every existing personal-memory tool retrieves *notes* and trusts the LLM to
    quote them faithfully. Memora retrieves *claims*: atomic factual statements
    extracted from your notes, each with a source-span pointer, validity window,
    and privacy band. Every LLM citation is architecturally validated against
    the source span — hallucinations are caught structurally, not via prompting.

    ## What it gives you

    - **Verified citations.** Hallucinated claim ids are stripped before the
      answer reaches you.
    - **Provenance with staleness propagation.** When you edit a note,
      downstream synthesis is auto-marked rotten.
    - **Time-aware reasoning.** Claims have validity windows; "what was true in
      March" actually works.
    - **Per-claim privacy.** Inline `secret` markers redact at the wire boundary
      for cloud LLMs.
    - **Active challenger.** A daily worker surfaces contradictions, stale
      dependencies, and frontier gaps in your world map.

    ## License

    Apache-2.0

Create CHANGELOG.md:

    # Changelog

    ## [Unreleased]

    ### Added
    - Initial project scaffolding.

Create Cargo.toml (workspace):

    [workspace]
    resolver = "2"
    members = [
        "crates/memora-core",
        "crates/memora-llm",
        "crates/memora-mcp",
        "crates/memora-cli",
    ]

    [workspace.package]
    version = "0.1.0-dev"
    edition = "2021"
    rust-version = "1.75"
    authors = ["Rado"]
    license = "Apache-2.0"
    repository = "https://github.com/<USER>/memora"
    readme = "README.md"

    [workspace.dependencies]
    anyhow = "1"
    thiserror = "1"
    tokio = { version = "1", features = ["rt-multi-thread", "fs", "io-util", "sync", "signal", "macros", "time"] }
    tracing = "0.1"
    tracing-subscriber = { version = "0.3", features = ["env-filter", "fmt"] }
    serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
    serde_json = "1"
    chrono = { version = "0.4", features = ["serde"] }
    async-trait = "0.1"
    blake3 = "1"
    rusqlite = { version = "0.31", features = ["bundled", "blob", "hooks"] }
    r2d2 = "0.8"
    r2d2_sqlite = "0.24"
    gray_matter = "0.2"
    pulldown-cmark = "0.10"
    walkdir = "2"
    notify = "6"
    regex = "1"
    hnsw_rs = "0.3"
    reqwest = { version = "0.12", default-features = false, features = ["json", "rustls-tls"] }
    secrecy = "0.8"
    clap = { version = "4", features = ["derive", "env"] }
    figment = { version = "0.10", features = ["toml", "env"] }
    toml = "0.8"
    tempfile = "3"
    mockito = "1"
    assert_cmd = "2"
    predicates = "3"
    uuid = { version = "1", features = ["v4"] }

    [profile.release]
    lto = "thin"
    codegen-units = 1
    strip = "symbols"

Replace `<USER>` in Cargo.toml with the GitHub username from `gh api user --jq .login`.

═══ STEP 4: CURSOR RULES ═══

Create .cursor/rules/memora-context.mdc:

    ---
    description: Memora project context — always loaded
    alwaysApply: true
    ---

    # Memora — Project Context

    You are an engineer working on Memora, a Rust-native verifiable cognitive
    memory engine for personal Obsidian-compatible vaults, served over MCP.

    ## Core mission
    Every claim the AI makes about a user's notes must be architecturally
    verifiable. Hallucinations are caught structurally, not via prompting.

    ## The wedge — Claim Graph, not Note Graph
    The atomic unit of memory is a CLAIM, not a NOTE. Every claim has:
    - subject, predicate, object
    - source_note + source_span (byte range in the markdown file)
    - span_fingerprint (blake3 of the source text)
    - valid_from, valid_until (temporal window)
    - privacy band (public | private | secret)
    - provenance edges to source claims when synthesized
    - relations: entails, contradicts, supersedes, derives, co_occurs

    LLM responses cite claim ids. The validator fetches each source span,
    recomputes the fingerprint, and rejects unverifiable citations.

    ## Stack invariants — DO NOT violate
    - Language: Rust 2021, MSRV 1.75
    - Storage: SQLite (FTS5 + JSON1). NEVER add Postgres, Neo4j, Mongo, Redis.
    - Vectors: HNSW via hnsw_rs. NEVER add Pinecone, Weaviate, Qdrant.
    - LLM providers: Anthropic, OpenAI, Ollama only, behind a single trait.
    - MCP: rmcp crate, stdio transport, primary interface.
    - Distribution: single static-ish binary. NEVER add Node, Python, or Java.
    - Markdown: Obsidian-compatible. Notes are user-owned, never rewritten
      destructively. The .memora/ sidecar holds all derived state.

    ## Privacy contract — never break
    - Notes default to privacy: private (configurable).
    - Inline markers <!--privacy:secret-->...<!--/privacy--> mark sub-spans.
    - Claims inherit privacy from notes; inline markers override.
    - secret claims sent to a cloud LLM MUST be redacted: subject preserved,
      predicate and object replaced with "[redacted]".

    ## Citation contract — never break
    - Every claim has a span_fingerprint computed at extraction time.
    - Validation re-reads the source span and re-computes the fingerprint.
    - LLM-emitted [claim:xxx] markers without a matching DB row are stripped.

    ## Forbidden patterns
    - println!/eprintln! in non-test code (use tracing macros)
    - unwrap()/expect() outside tests, build scripts, and main()
    - Storing user note bodies inside the SQLite DB (we store metadata, fts
      index, hashes — bodies stay in the markdown files)
    - Sending unredacted secret claims through any LLM client implementation
    - Adding crates with > 1MB of transitive dependencies without justification

    ## Style
    - anyhow::Result for application code, thiserror for library boundaries
    - tracing::{debug,info,warn,error} with structured fields
    - async fn over impl Future where idiomatic
    - Modules over giant files (split when a file exceeds ~400 lines)
    - Public items have rustdoc; complex items have examples
    - Tests live next to the code under #[cfg(test)] mod tests {}
    - Integration tests live in tests/

    ## When in doubt
    Choose the option that makes the citation contract harder to violate, the
    privacy contract harder to violate, and the binary smaller. In that order.

Create .cursor/rules/rust-standards.mdc:

    ---
    description: Rust style and idioms for Memora
    globs: ["**/*.rs"]
    ---

    # Rust Standards

    ## Errors
    - Library crates: define a domain error enum with thiserror.
    - Application crates (memora-cli, memora-mcp): use anyhow::{Result, Context}.
    - Always add context with .context("doing X")? at boundaries.
    - Never panic on user input. Validate, return Err.

    ## Async
    - tokio runtime, full features in workspace.
    - Spawn long-running work with tokio::spawn, but keep daemon ownership clear.
    - Cancellation: pass CancellationToken (tokio_util) for daemons.

    ## Logging
    - tracing macros with structured fields: tracing::info!(note_id = %id, "indexed").
    - One tracing_subscriber::fmt initialization in main.

    ## SQL
    - All schema changes go through migrations in memora-core/migrations/*.sql.
    - Use prepared statements, never string-concat queries.
    - Wrap multi-row writes in transactions.
    - Don't put bodies > 1KB in TEXT columns; reference paths instead.

    ## Tests
    - Unit: in module #[cfg(test)] block, no I/O, mock providers.
    - Integration: tests/it_*.rs, use tempfile for vault fixtures.
    - Async: #[tokio::test] not block_on.

    ## Performance defaults
    - Reuse connections via r2d2_sqlite pool.
    - Reuse HTTP client (reqwest::Client) — never construct per-call.
    - HNSW save() only on shutdown / scheduled checkpoint, not per-insert.

Create .cursor/rules/claim-graph.mdc:

    ---
    description: Claim Graph invariants — applies to claim handling code
    globs: ["**/claims/**/*.rs", "**/cite/**/*.rs", "**/extractor*.rs"]
    ---

    # Claim Graph Invariants

    ## Identity
    A claim id is blake3(format!("{s}|{p}|{o}|{note_id}|{span_start}"))
    truncated to 16 hex chars. Never let the LLM mint claim ids.

    ## Span fingerprint
    Computed as blake3(body[span_start..span_end]).
    Stored at extraction time. Recomputed at validation time.

    ## Extraction validation — done in Rust, not the LLM
    After receiving JSON from the extractor LLM, for each claim:
    1. Verify span_start < span_end and within body length.
    2. Verify the span contains non-whitespace, non-empty text.
    3. Reject if span < 5 chars or > 300 chars.
    4. Compute fingerprint, store.
    5. Reject claims with empty subject, predicate, or object.
    6. Reject claims whose fields are verbatim "..." or "[redacted]".

    ## Validity windows
    - valid_from is required (never null).
    - valid_until is null for "current".
    - When superseding: set old.valid_until = new.valid_from.
    - Never edit a claim's valid_from after creation.

    ## Privacy inheritance
    1. Note-level privacy is the floor.
    2. Inline markers override for spans inside.
    3. A claim's privacy = max(note_privacy, marker_privacy).
       Order: public < private < secret.

    ## Storage discipline
    - Never delete claims directly when a note is edited; delete-by-(note_id)
      and re-extract within a transaction.

    ## Embedding text
    The text embedded for a claim is format!("{} {} {}", s, p, o),
    lowercased, with multiple whitespace collapsed.

    ## Forbidden
    - Storing the source body inside a claim row.
    - Letting the extractor LLM specify a claim id (compute it in Rust).
    - Skipping span validation because "the LLM probably got it right".

Create .cursor/rules/citation-and-privacy.mdc:

    ---
    description: Citation and privacy enforcement
    globs: ["**/cite/**/*.rs", "**/privacy/**/*.rs", "**/mcp/**/*.rs"]
    ---

    # Citation Enforcement and Privacy Redaction

    ## Citation pipeline
    Every LLM response that goes to the user passes through:
    1. Parse [claim:HEX16] markers via regex \[claim:([a-f0-9]{16})\].
    2. For each marker: db.get_claim(id). If None → unverified.
    3. For each found claim: re-read note body, slice [span_start..span_end],
       blake3, compare to claim.span_fingerprint. If different → mismatch.
    4. If a quote precedes the marker, verify the quote is a substring
       (case-insensitive, whitespace-collapsed) of the source span.
    5. Output CitedAnswer { verified, unverified, mismatches }.

    ## Re-prompting policy
    If unverified or mismatches > 0:
    - Strip those sentences/markers.
    - Re-prompt with stricter system message listing only verified claim ids.
    - Cap retries at 1.
    - Return degraded: true if still unverified after retry.

    ## Privacy filter
    Inputs: claims + LlmDestination (Local | CloudKnown | CloudUnknown).
    - Local (Ollama): no redaction.
    - CloudKnown/CloudUnknown: redact secret claims — preserve subject,
      replace predicate and object with "[redacted]".

    The filter MUST run after retrieval and BEFORE prompt construction.
    Enforce by typing: PromptBuilder accepts &[RedactedClaim], not &[Claim].

    ## Logging discipline for privacy
    - Never log claim object/predicate at info level.
    - Use tracing::debug for content; default subscriber filter is INFO.
    - Gate any content-logging behind feature = "trace-secrets" (off by default).

    ## Forbidden
    - Silent swallowing of validator errors.
    - Reaching into the LLM provider directly from MCP handlers.
    - Logging contents of a claim with privacy = secret.

Create .cursor/rules/testing-and-commits.mdc:

    ---
    description: Testing and commit hygiene
    alwaysApply: true
    ---

    # Testing and Commit Hygiene

    ## Test layering
    - Unit tests inside the module: pure logic, mocked I/O.
    - Integration tests in tests/: spin up a temp vault, exercise public API.
    - End-to-end: spin up the MCP server in-process, send tool calls.
    - Mock LLMs with TestLlmClient that returns canned responses.

    ## Coverage targets
    - Every public function: at least one unit test.
    - Every MCP tool: at least one integration test.
    - Every privacy / citation rule: dedicated test that fails if rule is broken.

    ## Commit messages — Conventional Commits
    - feat(scope): ... | fix(scope): ... | refactor(scope): ... |
      test(scope): ... | docs(scope): ... | chore: ...
    - Scopes: core, llm, mcp, cli, claims, cite, privacy, consolidate, challenger

    ## When to commit
    - After each prompt's cargo test --all passes.
    - One commit per phase minimum.
    - Never commit broken tests or clippy failures.

    ## When to push
    - After every phase passes review.

    ## CI must pass for every PR
    - cargo fmt --check
    - cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
    - cargo test --all

═══ STEP 5: CI WORKFLOW ═══

Create .github/workflows/ci.yml:

    name: CI

    on:
      push:
        branches: [main]
      pull_request:

    jobs:
      test:
        name: Test on ${{ matrix.os }}
        runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
        strategy:
          fail-fast: false
          matrix:
            os: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest]
        steps:
          - uses: actions/checkout@v4
          - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
            with:
              components: rustfmt, clippy
          - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
          - name: fmt
            run: cargo fmt --all -- --check
          - name: clippy
            run: cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
          - name: test
            run: cargo test --all --all-features

Create .github/pr-templates/phase.md:

    ## Phase summary

    <!-- which phase, what changed at a high level -->

    ## Checklist
    - [ ] cargo fmt --check passes
    - [ ] cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes
    - [ ] cargo test --all passes
    - [ ] CHECKPOINT-N.md updated
    - [ ] No new forbidden patterns from .cursor/rules

═══ STEP 6: GIT INIT AND REMOTE ═══

Run:
- git init
- git branch -M main
- USERNAME=$(gh api user --jq .login)
- Replace `<USER>` placeholder in Cargo.toml with $USERNAME.
- gh repo create memora --public --source=. --remote=origin --description "Verifiable cognitive memory for personal vaults. Rust + SQLite + MCP-native."

If `gh repo create` fails with "already exists":
- gh repo set-default $USERNAME/memora
- git remote add origin git@github.com:$USERNAME/memora.git (if not present)

═══ STEP 7: INITIAL COMMIT AND PUSH ═══

- git add .
- git commit -m "chore: initial scaffolding with cursor rules and CI"
- git push -u origin main
